verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Early morning — 08:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

best.windFit: N 15 km/h · cross-onshore · poor wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 43 kJ, 9 s period, lully 21/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at low tide can still work on Belgas if the sandbars are lined up, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean fun-sized window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 26 kJ, 8 s period, lully 8/100, and good wind at 15 km/h.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; 6-9s at low tide is too dry and dumping for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; 6-9s at low tide is too dry and dumping for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.
ranked.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is leaning more serious than easy here.
warnings: 6-9s at low tide is too dry and dumping for Supertubos / Molhe Leste

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 66 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 77/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay; Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cantinho da Baia may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 16 kJ, 8 s period, lully 8/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size; Baleal may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
